reasoning: The short-term forecasts are dominated by tax seasonality. The benchmark date of April 12, 2026 falls right before the April 15 tax deadline. Historically, significant outflows from Retail Money Market Funds occur in the weeks following tax day (e.g., in 2025, WRMFNS fell from 2114.7 on Apr 14 to 2087.6 on Apr 28). This makes it highly likely that values on April 19 and May 12 will be lower than the April 12 baseline. By July and October, balances typically recover and grow, pushing the probability slightly above 0.5. Over longer horizons (1-10 years), uncertainty grows fundamentally: while nominal M2 trends persistently upward, a potential structurally lower interest rate policy (similar to the 2010s ZIRP era) could trigger heavy retail MMF outflows into bank deposits and risk assets. To accurately represent this ambiguity and align with the instruction to increase uncertainty over time, the long-term probabilities strictly converge toward 0.50.
